text.title: Common mode-mismatch failures
text.summary: Three production failure modes from picking the wrong constrained-decoding mode for the use case.

antipattern.description: Using `response_format={"type": "json_object"}` for extraction that downstream code expects to be schema-conformant.
antipattern.reason: json_object guarantees only that `json.loads()` succeeds — field names, types, enums, required-ness are all free. Pydantic parse fails on roughly every malformed batch and the failure is intermittent, not signal-rich.

antipattern.description: Modeling agent actions as a discriminated union under SO and dispatching on the discriminator field in user code.
antipattern.reason: Tool calls already give you the dispatch affordance natively (function name + typed args + provider-side validation + multi-tool selection). Doing it via SO discriminator forces a custom router that re-implements what the tool API already does and forfeits parallel tool calls.

antipattern.description: Forcing SQL, regex, GBNF, or other non-JSON output through a tool call argument as a single string field.
antipattern.reason: The model still has to generate the DSL inside an unconstrained string — no grammar mask applies inside string values. Use a real grammar (Outlines/XGrammar/GBNF) on a model that supports it; XGrammar reports 100x throughput vs string-inside-JSON workarounds.

example.note: No grammar applies inside the string; you get LLM-flavoured SQL with the same syntax errors as plain prompting.
